summary: Persistent SYSTEM/root service with unpinned auto-update and remote IM-controlled command execution
details: On npm postinstall, the package registers a persistent service running as SYSTEM on Windows (via node-windows with sc.exe failure restart/0/restart/0/restart/0 and start=auto) or as root via systemd. The service forks a worker that (a) every 6 hours runs `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@<latest>` with no version pin, hash check, or signature verification, then restarts to load the new code (service/updater.js:107), and (b) connects to a hardcoded vendor IM channel (Rongcloud appKey `bmdehs6pbyyks`, token fetched from `https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im/api/claw/token/<nodeId>`) and accepts JSON command messages that spawn shell commands via `spawn('bash', ['-c', command])` (service/modules/script-executor.js:139), run start/stop/restart.sh/.bat scripts, uninstall the local service, and delete `~/.claw-bridge/config.json` (service/modules/rongyun-message-handler.js handleCommand / handleDeviceControl). The combination gives the author (or anyone who compromises the npm publisher account or the Rongcloud appKey/account routing) elevated remote code execution on every installed host: either by publishing a new version that the auto-updater pulls within 6 hours, or by sending an IM message to the enrolled node id. The README documents the remote-control design, but installation enrolls the host into a third-party control plane at SYSTEM/root, which is a backdoor by behavior regardless of disclosure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
